LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ACE UNDERWRITING AGENCIES LIMITED FOR AND ON BEHALF OF SYNDICATE 2488'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF THIS DATE BY JUDGE HAROLD KAHN. COURT ISSUES TENTATIVE FINDINGS AND REASONS IN OPEN FORUM. THE COURT INDICATES IT WOULD GRANT THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IF THE LOSS ISSUE WAS THE ONLY ISSUE, BUT IT ISN'T THE ONLY ISSUE BEFORE THE COURT. THE COURT HEARS FROM EACH SIDE. EACH SIDE IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDERS THAT THEY BELIEVE ARE CONSISTENT WITH THE COURT'S VIEW ON THE LOSS ISSUE, WHICH IS THAT A LOSS DEPENDS ON WHETHER THERE WAS AN OBLIGATION ON THE PART OF NMI TO PAY FOR THE DEFENSE AND INDEMNITY OF THE MARKS ACTION. THE COURT GIVES GUIDELINES FOR OTHER LANGUAGE IN THE [PROPOSED] ORDERS AS SET FORTH INTHE TRANSCRIPT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NMI HOLDINGS, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE RUNOFF ENDORSEMENT IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO THE PROFFERED INTERPRETATION BY THE INSURERS. SEE TRANSCRIPT FOR OTHER FINDINGS AND DETAILS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Notice Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Ace Underwriting Agencies Limited For And On Behalf Of Syndicate 2488s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, John D. Green and Erica Villanueva of the law firm FARELLA BRAUN & MARTEL LLP (415.954.4400) appear as counsels for plaintiff/cross-defendant/opposing party, NMI Holdings, Inc.

Attorney, Michael L. Fox of law firm Sedgwick LLP (415.781.7900) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/moving party, Ace Underwriting Agencies, Ltd.

Attorney, George Gigounas of law firm DLA Piper (415.615.6005) appears as counsel for defendant/joining party, Beazley PLC

Attorney, Benjamin C. Eggert of law firm Wiley Rein LLP (202.719.7336) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/joining party, Catlin Specialty Insurance Company

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANT Ace Underwriting Agencies Limited For And On Behalf Of Syndicate 2488's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION as of this date by Judge Harold Kahn. Court issues tentative findings and reasons in open forum. The Court indicates it would grant the motion for judgment on the pleadings with leave to amend if the loss issue was the only issue, but it isn't the only issue before the Court. The Court hears from each side. Each side is directed to submit [proposed] written orders that they believe are consistent with the Court's view on the loss issue, which is that a loss depends on whether there was an obligation on the part of NMI to pay for the defense and indemnity of the Marks action. The Court gives guidelines for other language in the [proposed] orders as set forth inthe transcript.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEFENDANT BEAZLEY FURLONGE LTD'S Joinder Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Ace Underwriting Agencies Limited's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION as of this date by Judge Harold Kahn. Court issues tentative findings and reasons in open forum. The Court indicates it would grant the motion for judgment on the pleadings with leave to amend if the loss issue was the only issue, but it isn't the only issue before the Court. The Court hears from each side. Each side is directed to submit [proposed] written orders that they believe are consistent with the Court's view on the loss issue, which is that a loss depends on whether there was an obligation on the part of NMI to pay for the defense and indemnity of the Marks action. The Court gives guidelines for other language in the [proposed] orders as set forth inthe transcript.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Cross-Motion And Cross-Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, John D. Green and Erica Villanueva of the law firm FARELLA BRAUN & MARTEL LLP (415.954.4400) appear as counsels for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party, NMI Holdings, Inc.

Attorney, Michael L. Fox of law firm Sedgwick LLP (415.781.7900) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party, Ace Underwriting Agencies, Ltd.

Attorney, George Gigounas of law firm DLA Piper (415.615.6005) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Beazley PLC

Attorney, Benjamin C. Eggert of law firm Wiley Rein LLP (202.719.7336) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party, Catlin Specialty Insurance Company

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court modifies the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF NMI HOLDINGS, INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED on the grounds that the runoff endorsement is reasonably susceptible to the proffered interpretation by the insurers. See transcript for other findings and details. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Joinder To Defendant And Cross-Complainant Ace Underwriting Agencies Limited For And On Behalf Of Syndicate 2488s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, John D. Green and Erica Villanueva of the law firm FARELLA BRAUN & MARTEL LLP (415.954.4400) appear as counsels for plaintiff/cross-defendant/opposing party, NMI Holdings, Inc.

Attorney, Michael L. Fox of law firm Sedgwick LLP (415.781.7900) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant Ace Underwriting Agencies, Ltd.

Attorney, George Gigounas of law firm DLA Piper (415.615.6005) appears as counsel for defendant/joining party, Beazley PLC

Attorney, Benjamin C. Eggert of law firm Wiley Rein LLP (202.719.7336) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/moving party, Catlin Specialty Insurance Company

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANT CATLIN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Joinder To Defendant And Cross-Complainant Ace Underwriting Agencies Limited For And On Behalf Of Syndicate 2488's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION as of this date by Judge Harold Kahn. Court issues tentative findings and reasons in open forum. The Court indicates it would grant the motion for judgment on the pleadings with leave to amend if the loss issue was the only issue, but it isn't the only issue before the Court. The Court hears from each side. Each side is directed to submit [proposed] written orders that they believe are consistent with the Court's view on the loss issue, which is that a loss depends on whether there was an obligation on the part of NMI to pay for the defense and indemnity of the Marks action. The Court gives guidelines for other language in the [proposed] orders as set forth inthe transcript.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
